title: Senior Product Marketing Manager
required_skills: 7+ years product marketing experience (B2B tech preferred)
Storytelling and simplifying complex concepts for multiple audiences
Influencing senior stakeholders and aligning cross-functional teams
Data-driven approach — using metrics to shape narratives and guide decisions
Value proposition development and customer-centric messaging
Sales enablement (battle cards, one-pagers, solution briefs)
bonus_skills: Master's degree in Business, Marketing or related field
B2B travel technology / travel distribution domain knowledge
matched_skills: Data-driven analytics (KPI framework design, campaign performance optimisation)
Market Strategy & Insights / value proposition alignment
Market segmentation & intelligence (customer feedback and insights)
missing_skills: 7+ years dedicated product marketing experience (CV shows ~4 years, non-product-marketing titles)
B2B travel technology / travel distribution domain expertise
Sales enablement asset creation (battle cards, solution briefs, one-pagers)
Proven senior stakeholder influence and matrixed org alignment
core_responsibilities: Own and refine value propositions aligned to customer needs and business priorities
Build customer-centric messaging frameworks and ensure channel consistency
Develop sales enablement materials (battle cards, one-pagers, solution briefs, FAQs)
Gather structured customer and market feedback to iterate messaging and GTM strategy
Drive and coordinate cross-functional GTM initiatives to increase product adoption
recommended_emphasis: Highlight GTM strategy contributions at Minth Group (Market Intelligence Specialist) — position sizing, competitive analysis, launch support
Emphasise C-level data storytelling examples from HSBC PMO role — translate complex data into executive narratives
Showcase cross-functional coordination experience from Hapag-Lloyd (customer experience project spanning multiple teams)
Quantify any campaign or product messaging impact (conversion uplift, adoption metrics, revenue influenced)
Stress multi-market (EMEA, APAC) coverage to compensate for lack of travel tech vertical experience
